rationale: Add a small procedural config to prefer file-based IO to reduce Windows CLI arg-length risk without touching other files.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedural_rule.v1",
  "rule_id": "prefer_file_io_for_large_payloads",
  "description": "When payloads are large, write to temp files and pass file paths instead of long CLI args.",
  "applies_to": ["windows"],
  "threshold_bytes": 4096,
  "actions": ["write_payload_to_file", "pass_path_arg"],
  "notes": "Use this to avoid command line length limits on Windows."
}

risks: Low. Adds a new procedural rule file only.
expected_benefit: Reduces risk of Windows CLI arg-length failures by encouraging file-based payload passing.
